task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
trace: To calculate the median, the function first checks if the input list `nums` is empty and returns `None` if it is. If not, it sorts the list and calculates the middle index. If the length of the list is even, it computes the average of the two middle numbers and returns that as a float. If the length is odd, it simply returns the middle number as a float. This approach ensures the function handles both odd and even lengths correctly and efficiently.
